planBuildShip: Plan, build, and ship — with a team of AI agents instead of one.
pageSubtitle: Linear-style design prototypes for openteams. Single chromatic accent, four-step surface ladder, hairline borders, mono in all numbers. Toggle between dark and light at the top right.
pt1Desc: Used 80% of the time. Multi-model DAG with monogram avatars, cost per node, smart routing on. Notice: no chromatic encoding per model — monograms carry identity, lavender carries action.
pt2Title: Prototype 02: Main workspace · Free chat mode
pt2Desc: For 70% of the day — quick asks, bug fixes, 'thinking out loud' moments. Multiple agents in one shared session, @-mentioning each other, all context visible. One-tap promotion to workflow when things get complex.
pt3Desc: Two critical conversion moments. Onboarding: 30 seconds from install to a team auto-configured for your use case. Pro page: anti-sales tone, Free forever complete promise, early-bird scarcity that's real.
pt4Desc: Linear modals don't use drop shadows for elevation — they sit on a scrim overlay above the canvas, with their own hairline border doing the lifting. Two patterns: a destructive confirmation, and a multi-field creation form.
pt5Desc: Two-column settings: left nav with grouped sections, right form panel. Linear's pattern — dense form rows separated by hairlines, switch toggles in lavender when on, no shadows anywhere.
pt6Desc: Linear dropdowns: hairline-bordered panel above the trigger, optional search bar, grouped options, monospace keyboard hints in footer. Hovered row uses surface-2 background, selected row gets a primary checkmark.
ptTokensDesc: These swatches live-update with the mode toggle. The four-step surface ladder is what carries hierarchy — never shadow, never atmospheric gradients.
askAnything: Ask anything — @claude fix this bug, @codex add tests…
discussionPlaceholder: Discuss with @lead, redirect a step, or @ any member…
smartRoutingDesc: Auto-picks best model per task. Saved $4.20 this week.
anyQuestionReplyPlaceholder: @anyone to add to this session, or just reply…
onboardSub: We'll set up the right AI team for you. Change anything later.
smartRoutingTip: Smart routing picks the best model for each task automatically
skipOnboard: Skip — I'll build my own team
freeProSub: Free gives you multi-agent. Pro gives you the data to make it worth it.
earlyBirdText: first 100 indie devs lock $9/mo for life · {left} left
ROIInsight: Model ROI dashboard · which model is worth it
smartRoutingV2: Smart routing v2 · learns from your data
configImportExport: Config export/import · setup syncs, code stays local
freeNeverDowngraded: Free tier will never be downgraded to push Pro
dialogSubRetry: Steps 3, 4, and 5 will be re-run. Completed work in step 1 and 2 will be preserved. Estimated cost: $0.42.
dialogSubNewTask: Describe the project. We'll pick the right team for you.
detailsPlaceholder: Add monthly + annual plans. Use Stripe Checkout (not Elements). Test mode keys only.
smartRoutingRecommend: Smart routing recommends a 4-person team for this scope
connectAgentsExisting: Connect the AI agents you already use. openteams will route tasks across them.
keyStorageTip: API keys are stored encrypted in your local keychain. They never leave your machine.
defaultSmartRoutingSub: Auto-pick the best model per task based on context
showCostNodeSub: Display dollar cost on each workflow step
whyThisModelSub: Reveal routing logic next to each agent assignment
warnOverDollarSub: Confirm before kicking off expensive workflows
designTokensSub: These swatches live-update with the mode toggle. The four-step surface ladder is what carries hierarchy — never shadow, never atmospheric gradients.
footerText: openteams · Linear-style design system · prototypes for product spec ch.6
discussPlaceholder: Discuss with the team... @claude to explain, or kick-off...
turnWorkflowSuccess: Successfully mapped current discussion context into a 5-step DAG workflow plan!
addMemberDesc: Provision a custom AI avatar agent role in your openteams space
addProviderDesc: Configure an external LLM endpoint proxy credentials

roiDashboardSub: Stats over the past 30 days comparing line count, bug-squashing success, and compute spend
roiDesc: System adaptation rules evaluate that Claude has a highly effective codebase ROI for architectural rewrites.

agents.config.schema.common.append_promp: Extra text appended to the prompt.
agents.config.schema.common.base_command: Override the base command with a custom command.
agents.config.schema.common.additional_p: Additional parameters to append to the base command.
agents.config.schema.common.env.label: Environment variables
agents.config.schema.common.env.descript: Environment variables to set when running the executor.
agents.config.schema.common.model.label: Model
agents.config.schema.common.model.descri: Model or profile used by this agent.
agents.config.schema.common.yolo.label: YOLO mode
agents.config.schema.common.yolo.descrip: Run with fewer approval prompts where the executor supports it.
agents.config.schema.common.auto_compact: Enable auto-compaction when the context approaches the model context limit.
agents.config.schema.common.variant.desc: Executor variant or preset to use.
agents.config.schema.common.agent.label: Agent
agents.config.schema.common.claude_code_: Route Claude Code execution through the configured router.
agents.config.schema.common.plan.label: Plan mode
agents.config.schema.common.plan.descrip: Enable planning behavior for Claude Code runs.
agents.config.schema.common.approvals.de: Require approval prompts for Claude Code actions.
agents.config.schema.common.effort.label: Effort
agents.config.schema.common.effort.descr: Reasoning effort or execution effort level.
agents.config.schema.common.dangerously_: Skip Claude Code permission checks. Use only in trusted workspaces.
agents.config.schema.common.disable_api_: Run without injecting the configured API key.
agents.config.schema.common.ask_for_appr: Controls when Codex asks the user to approve actions.
agents.config.schema.common.oss.label: OSS mode
agents.config.schema.common.oss.descript: Use the open-source Codex mode when available.
agents.config.schema.common.model_reason: Reasoning effort for the underlying model.
Summary style for model reasoning output.
agents.config.schema.common.base_instruc: Base instruction text passed to the agent.
agents.config.schema.common.include_appl: Expose the apply_patch tool to the agent when supported.
agents.config.schema.common.model_provid: Provider configuration used for the selected model.
agents.config.schema.common.compact_prom: Prompt text used when compacting context.
agents.config.schema.common.developer_in: Additional developer-level instructions for the agent.
agents.config.schema.common.allow_all_to: Allow Copilot to use all available tools.
agents.config.schema.common.allow_tool.d: Tool name or pattern to explicitly allow.
agents.config.schema.common.deny_tool.de: Tool name or pattern to explicitly deny.
agents.config.schema.common.add_dir.desc: Extra directories the agent may access.
agents.config.schema.common.disable_mcp_: MCP server names to disable for this agent.
agents.config.schema.common.force.label: Force allow
agents.config.schema.common.force.descri: Force allow commands unless explicitly denied.
agents.config.schema.common.autonomy.des: Permission level for file and system operations.
agents.config.schema.common.reasoning_ef: Reasoning effort level for this agent.
agents.config.schema.common.thinking_eff: Per-run thinking effort or token budget.
agents.config.schema.common.dangerously_: Allow all commands to execute, even if they are unsafe.
agents.config.schema.amp.model.descripti: AMP mode profile. Maps to CLI --mode: smart, deep, rush, or free.
agents.config.schema.droid.model.descrip: Model to use, such as gpt-5.3-codex, Claude Sonnet, Claude Opus, or GLM.
agents.config.schema.droid.reasoning_eff: Reasoning effort level: none, dynamic, off, low, medium, or high.
agents.config.schema.gemini.model.descri: Gemini model to use, such as gemini-2.5-pro or gemini-3-pro-preview.
agents.config.schema.gemini.thinking_eff: Per-run Gemini thinking effort: off, low, medium, high, max, or a numeric budget.
agents.config.schema.qwen_code.model.des: Qwen Code model to use, such as qwen3-coder-plus or qwen3-coder-flash.
agents.config.schema.qwen_code.thinking_: Per-run Qwen Code reasoning effort: off, low, medium, high, max, or a numeric token budget.
Cursor model profile, such as auto, Sonnet, GPT-5, Opus, Grok, or Composer.
agents.config.schema.codex.ask_for_appro: Controls when Codex asks for approval: unless trusted, on failure, on request, or never.
